reachability_artifacts: built sworn ship and sworn mark-shipped runs against one temporary deployed-release history proving read-only readiness, origin-fetch/ancestry blocking, exact status metadata plus Baton ship/status/index transition with byte-identical pure-plan board in one local commit, unchanged remote, exact push-and-cleanup handoff, the recorded legacy no-release-merge-identity containment skip, and the fully regated no-deployment-evidence already-shipped nothing-to-do rerun
release_benefit: Operators can prove readiness before cutover and record deployment afterward without conflating or weakening either fact.

design_decisions.choice: Keep pre-cutover readiness and the post-deployment shipped transition as distinct public operations.
options: ship remains read-only and mark-shipped records deployment
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-15: preserve sworn ship as the readiness gate and add sworn mark-shipped as the revalidated post-deployment transition.
design_decisions.rationale: Safe to deploy and actually deployed are different auditable facts and must not collapse into one success signal.

design_decisions.choice: Persist deployment truth in the exact Baton v0.15 ship block with shipped_at, deployed_commit, deploy_ref, and /mark-shipped identity.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified under Brad's 2026-07-16 instruction to proceed with exact Baton v0.15 conformance: the native command and installed /mark-shipped role must write the same durable shape.
design_decisions.rationale: A second Sworn-only deployment object would make the native command disagree with the exact Baton role installed by the same release.

design_decisions.choice: Keep mark-shipped local-only and hand the exact integration push command to the human operator.
options: one local bookkeeping commit plus human push handoff
external tooling edits status without a local command
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified under Brad's 2026-07-16 instruction to proceed with the orchestrator recommendation.
design_decisions.rationale: Exact Baton v0.15 assigns mark-shipped local bookkeeping authority, not network publication or deployment authority.

positive_scenarios: Ship is read-only and mark-shipped fetches/validates origin, records exact Baton status metadata plus ship/status/index transition with byte-identical pure-plan board in one local commit, leaves remotes unchanged, emits the exact handoff, and later returns the fully regated already-shipped no-op.
negative_scenarios: Pre-cutover never marks deployment; origin fetch failure, local-behind/divergent history, stale readiness, missing deploy containment, dirty/wrong-branch worktree, unresolved release merge, or any automatic push/cleanup never changes terminal state or passes the no-op gate.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Separating safe-to-deploy from deployed keeps release history truthful while retaining one conservative evidence gate.
